title: Sunnyvale → Lake Tahoe · 3 Days
subtitle: Sunnyvale → South Lake Tahoe (US-50) → around the lake → back via Truckee & I-80

days.title: Sunnyvale → South Lake Tahoe (via US-50)

stops.name: Sunnyvale (start)
stops.note: Fuel up; Sacramento and Placerville have the last cheap gas before the climb.

stops.name: Apple Hill, Placerville
stops.note: October = apple/harvest season; orchards, cider, pie. Weekends get crowded — go early.

stops.name: Heavenly Village, South Lake Tahoe
stops.note: Arrive, check in, walk the lakefront at Lakeview Commons.

fuelCharging.name: Fuel @ Placerville, CA
fuelCharging.note: Last good-value fuel before US-50 climbs to Echo Summit (7,382 ft).

risks: US-50 climbs over Echo Summit (~7,400 ft). By mid-October an early snowstorm is possible — check Caltrans QuickMap and carry chains; R2 chain controls can appear on short notice.
Leave by ~9am to clear the Sacramento area before traffic and reach Apple Hill before the weekend crowd.

days.title: Around the lake: Emerald Bay & East Shore
days.timezoneNote: East shore (Sand Harbor) is in Nevada — same Pacific time, but you cross the state line

stops.name: Emerald Bay / Vikingsholm
stops.note: Iconic overlook; steep 1-mi trail down to Vikingsholm castle if you want the legs workout.

stops.name: Eagle Falls Trailhead
stops.note: Short hike to the falls / Eagle Lake. Tiny lot — arrive before 9am or it's full.

stops.name: Sand Harbor, NV (Lake Tahoe NV State Park)
stops.note: Best swimming/boulders & clearest water. Parking lot closes when full (often mid-morning summer; easier in Oct).

meal.name: Picnic at Sand Harbor or lunch in Tahoe City

risks: Emerald Bay's parking pullouts are small and fill fast on weekends — have a plan B viewpoint.
October water is cold and afternoons get windy; mornings are calmer for the lake.

days.title: North Shore → Truckee → Sunnyvale (I-80)

stops.name: Tahoe City
stops.note: Coffee + the Truckee River outlet; quick stop before heading to I-80.

stops.name: Donner Memorial State Park, Truckee
stops.note: Donner Lake, visitor center & the Pioneer Monument — history of Donner Pass.

fuelCharging.name: Fuel @ Truckee, CA
fuelCharging.note: Top up before the Donner Pass descent and the long I-80 run west.

risks: Donner Pass (I-80, ~7,000 ft) is the Sierra's biggest snow magnet. A mid-October storm can trigger chain controls or brief closures — check Caltrans before leaving Tahoe and don't push into an active storm.
Sunday afternoon I-80 westbound from Tahoe is the classic Bay Area return jam. Leave Tahoe by late morning to beat it.

bookingCountdown.note: Tahoe weekends sell out and surge-price; lock a refundable rate early.
Seasonal tours often end by late Sept/Oct — confirm it's still running.

bookingCountdown.item: Campground (if camping instead): D.L. Bliss / Emerald Bay = ReserveCalifornia; Fallen Leaf = Recreation.gov
bookingCountdown.note: California STATE parks use ReserveCalifornia, NOT Recreation.gov. Both release ~6 months out. Many Tahoe campgrounds close for the season by mid-Oct.

items.label: State park day-use fees (Eagle Falls / Sand Harbor / Donner)
Misc (parking, cider at Apple Hill, snacks)

tips: No national park here, so the $80 America the Beautiful pass is NOT needed — but Tahoe ringed by California + Nevada STATE parks (separate day-use fees). Carry a bit of cash/card for lot kiosks.

Mid-October is shoulder season: smaller crowds and fall color, but the first Sierra snow can arrive any time. Watch Caltrans QuickMap for chain controls on US-50 (Echo Summit) and I-80 (Donner Pass), and keep chains in the trunk.
Sand Harbor and Eagle Falls have tiny lots that fill by mid-morning even off-season — do the popular stops early, relax in the afternoon.
Going up US-50 and back via I-80/Truckee makes a natural loop so you see both the south and north shores without backtracking.
Cell coverage is patchy on US-50 over the summit and around the west shore — download offline maps for the whole loop.
disclaimer: This itinerary was assembled by an AI from general knowledge and may be out of date. Sierra weather, road/chain controls, park hours and fees, and lodging prices change fast — confirm with official sources (Caltrans QuickMap, the park sites, ReserveCalifornia/Recreation.gov, your nav app) before and during your trip.
